text.title: Win-Loss Interview Program Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Win-Loss Interview Program application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Interview ratio 50% won + 50% lost. Lost-only = pessimism bias; won-only = sales-narrative confirmation.
rule.rationale: Industry data: balanced sample produces materially better insights than either-only.

rule.statement: Interviewer is NOT the rep who worked the deal. Otherwise interviewees give socially-desirable responses.
rule.rationale: Interviewer's relationship affects honesty; separating interviewer from seller is the bias control.

rule.statement: Script includes: 'what alternatives did you consider?', 'what would have made you go the other way?', 'what surprised you?' Open-ended + evidence-anchored.
rule.rationale: Leading questions ('was our price the issue?') corrupt signal; open + anchored questions retrieve actual reasons.

rule.statement: Interview within 30 days of close. After 30, memory degrades + rationalization grows.
rule.rationale: Memory + emotion decay sharply post-30-days; interviews after that point yield mostly rationalization.

rule.statement: Quarterly synthesis distributed to PM (product), marketing (messaging), sales (objection-handling). Without distribution, insights die.
rule.rationale: Single-channel synthesis = limited impact; three-channel ensures insights become action.
